heading: Response
body:
Each method returns a `Document` object or a cursor that contains
the response from the database after the command has been executed. Each
database command performs a different function, so the response content
can vary across commands. However, every response contains documents
with the following fields:

| Field             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| \<command result> | Provides fields specific to the database command. For example, count returns the n field and explain returns the queryPlanner field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ok                | Indicates whether the command has succeeded (1) or failed (0).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| operationTime     | Indicates the logical time of the operation. MongoDB uses the logical time to order operations. To learn more about logical time, see our blog post about the Global Logical Clock.                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| $clusterTime      | Provides a document that returns the signed cluster time. Cluster time is a logical time used for ordering of operations.The document contains the following fields:clusterTime, which is the timestamp of the highest known cluster time for the member.signature, which is a document that contains the hash of the cluster time and the ID of the key used to sign the cluster time. |
